reason: SPURIOUS ARGUMENT: |det Λ|=1 AND δ^(4) ARE IRRELEVANT TO THE DISTRIBUTIONAL DEFINITION — The proof's parenthetical remark states: 'One also uses |det Λ| = 1, which ensures that the four-dimensional delta function δ^(4) is a Lorentz scalar.' But the distributional Definition 2 (eq. Tmunu-dist-def, line 70-74) does NOT contain any delta function. It defines T^{αβ} via a proper-time integral ∫ p^α γ̇^β φ(γ(τ)) dτ. The delta function δ^(4)(x-γ(τ)) appears only in the INFORMAL representation eq. Tmunu-delta4-add (line 124-128), which the text itself describes as 'Informally, Definition 2 says that...' Therefore the argument about |det Λ| ensuring δ^(4) is a scalar is (a) logically unnecessary for the actual distributional proof, and (b) conflates the rigorous distributional definition with the informal δ-function notation. In the proper distributional proof, no |det Λ| factor arises because the integral ∫...dτ contains no spacetime volume element — dτ is a one-dimensional line integral along the worldline. The |det Λ| = 1 fact would only be needed if one were proving that the formal expression with δ^(4) transforms correctly under x → Λx, which would require showing δ^(4)(Λx - Λγ) = δ^(4)(Λ(x-γ)) = |det Λ|^{-1} δ^(4)(x-γ) = δ^(4)(x-γ). But this is the informal argument the addendum was supposed to REPLACE with rigour.
